proof_boundary: This kit defines hosted smoke commands, owner inputs, acceptance criteria, and evidence fields. It is not hosted/live proof until the commands are run after owner-approved deploy state and secrets, with redacted logs/screenshots attached.

required_owner_inputs.reason: Proves which deployed web surface is in scope for screenshots and browser smoke.
Binds hosted proof to a repo commit or release rather than an unknown live state.
Shows required variables are present without exposing secret values.
Defines whether scripts can create disposable users or must use owner-provided test accounts.
Ensures evidence is attachable without credentials or private data.

acceptance_gates.pass_condition: Each evidence row records hosted URL, deploy id or commit, timestamp, command, and operator.
Logs and screenshots contain no API keys, bearer tokens, session cookies, passwords, or direct personal contact/payment identifiers.
At least access, auth, schema, strategist, insights, retrieval/analyze, and pricing/entitlement checks are attached before buyer-safe hosted claims.
Every failed smoke row records whether the blocker is secret/config, deploy drift, schema/RLS, provider reliability, payment, or product behavior.
Hosted proof upgrades only the specific checked workflow; it does not upgrade prediction-accuracy or commercial-ready claims by itself.

external_framework_alignment.requirement: Verify software behavior and security outcomes with evidence before release claims.
Treat secure deployment, least privilege, and observable failure modes as product responsibilities.

external_framework_alignment.framework: NIST AI RMF Measure and Manage
external_framework_alignment.requirement: Measure AI-system behavior in deployment-relevant conditions and manage residual risk before external claims.
